commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `getDefaultSessionDir` to percent-escape path separators and adds a `continueRecent` check that starts a fresh session when the selected session header cwd differs from the requested cwd.

PR surface: Source +16. Total +16 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main maps the example cwd pair to the same default session directory and `continueRecent` opens the newest valid file without matching `header.cwd`. I did not run an artifact-writing repro because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof is missing and maintainers need to pick or repair the upgrade-safe canonical fix among several open candidates; this should not queue automation from this read-only review.

target.title: fix(session): percent-encode path separators in session dir to prevent cross-project collision
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`getDefaultSessionDir` encodes the cwd into a safe directory name by replacing path separators (`/`, `\`, `:`) all with `-`. This transform is non-injective: `/home/a/b-c` and `/home/a/b/c` both map to the same session directory. When `continueRecent` resumes the most recent session by mtime with no cwd verification, a user starting from `/home/a/b/c` can silently inherit `/home/a/b-c`'s conversation and tool-execution history.

## Why This Change Was Made

The encoding now uses URI percent-encoding (`%2F`, `%5C`, `%3A`) for path separators, making it injective. `continueRecent` also verifies the session header cwd matches the requested cwd as defense-in-depth.

## Evidence

- Two distinct cwds differing only by a path separator (`client/app` vs `client-app`) now produce different session directories
- `continueRecent` from one project starts a fresh session when the header cwd does not match the requested cwd
- TypeScript compilation verified clean

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: krissding
